title: Passport a valid document for SIR identification, say Election Commission officials
content:
• The Election Commission of India (ECI) confirmed that Indian passports remain one of the 12 valid documents for voters to establish eligibility on electoral rolls during the special intensive revision (SIR).
• The Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) clarified that an Indian passport is a travel document issued under the Passport Act of 1967, and not conclusive proof of citizenship, which is governed by the Citizenship Act, 1955.
• Government sources emphasized that no new policy regarding passports as proof of citizenship has been implemented by the Modi government in the past 12 years.
